raw_consumer_statement:
We sold to a XXXX called XXXX XXXX Tablets for total amount of XXXX. 
We provided them with an invoice and stated on that all the terms and conditions. 
XXXX agreed to the terms and signed the invoice. They provided their credit card so we can charge the invoice value. This happened on XX/XX/year>. But on XX/XX/year> XXXX disputed the charges we had done so we got a chargeback from our XXXX cartd processor. They put the reason for disput that they did not receive the product. We sent to our XXXX the signed invoice that clearly stated that XXXX was not responssible for delivery and that the buyer was supposed to pick up the product. The term also stated that there would be no refund. Our XXXX sent our respone with the documents to American Express stating that the card holder can not dispute as there was no refund. After going back and forth for couple of weeks American Express sided with the card holder and closed the dispute. 
American Express clearly has seen the signed invoice and agreement with our terms by the card holder yet they decided to favour their customer. This is totally unethical And they should not have done that.
